evals.prompt: I'm building a new skill for ADHD executive function support — it should help with task prioritization, time blindness compensation, and working memory scaffolding. I need it to work across Claude, OpenAI, n8n, and as an MCP server. Can you scaffold the full project with the build system?
evals.expected_output: A complete project scaffold: source/manifest.json with metadata wrapper and JSON Schema parameters, build.ts generating all 6 formats, package.json with per-format scripts, and source markdown stubs.

evals.prompt: I have an existing dyscalculia-support-skill project. Its manifest uses the older flat format with CommandParameter arrays. I want to migrate it to the standardized manifest schema and regenerate the build.ts.
evals.expected_output: Migration of flat manifest to metadata-wrapper + JSON Schema parameters. New domain-agnostic build.ts. All 6 formats build.

evals.prompt: I've got a tarot card reading interpretation skill. It has commands for single-card pulls, three-card spreads, Celtic Cross readings, and card-of-the-day. Generate the multi-format build system for it.
evals.expected_output: Complete build system for a non-educational domain. build.ts must be domain-agnostic. Manifest with metadata, skills, and commands with JSON Schema parameters.

evals.prompt: I have a dyslexia compliance audit skill that currently works in English for the US (IDEA/Section 504). I need to add Spanish and French locales — the Spanish version covers LOMLOE/ACNEAE and the French version covers French education law. Localize the full build so each locale generates all 6 output formats with translated content and display strings.
evals.expected_output: Manifest updated with defaultLocale: 'en' and locales: ['en', 'es', 'fr']. Locale override files created at source/locales/es.json and source/locales/fr.json with translated metadata, skill, and command display strings. Locale markdown subdirectories created (source/skills/es/, source/skills/fr/, source/commands/es/, source/commands/fr/) with stubs or translated content. build.ts is the standard locale-aware template (domain-agnostic, no hardcoded strings). package.json includes build:locale:es and build:locale:fr scripts. Build produces dist/en/, dist/es/, dist/fr/ directories each containing all 6 formats. Fallback works: missing locale files resolve to English default. --locale flag works for single-locale builds.

evals.prompt: I have an existing single-locale skill (no locales in the manifest). I want to add German and Japanese locales to it. Walk me through the migration without breaking the existing build.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step migration: add defaultLocale and locales to metadata, create source/locales/ directory with de.json and ja.json override files, create locale subdirectories for skills and commands, add build:locale scripts. Backward compatibility preserved: existing default-locale content stays in place as the fallback. Build output changes from dist/<format>/ to dist/en/<format>/, dist/de/<format>/, dist/ja/<format>/.

iteration_1.eval_2_dyscalculia: 9/10 — domain term leaked into CLI section of build.ts
iteration_1.eval_3_tarot: 9/10 — domain term leaked into CLI section of build.ts
iteration_1.fix_applied: Strengthened SKILL.md and build-template.md with explicit domain-agnostic guidance for CLI generator

iteration_2.eval_4_i18n_compliance: pending — new eval for multi-locale build
iteration_2.eval_5_locale_migration: pending — new eval for adding locales to existing project
iteration_2.fix_applied: Added locale support to manifest schema, build template, and SKILL.md
